name: app-shell
title: AppShell
description: Site navigation shell: SkipLink + sticky header (brand · primary NavBar · header actions with a ThemeToggle) + main content region + footer. Router-agnostic — the nav-links slot ships plain NavBar.Link anchors with the asChild router recipe in the slot comment.

files.destinationHint: app layout dir (src/app/ or src/components/layout/); keep the three files together — AppShell.tsx imports ./useTheme and ./app-shell.css

files.skipIf: the app already has a theme controller — then rewire ThemeToggle (or replace it) in the header-actions slot

files.destinationHint: next to AppShell.tsx; imported by AppShell.tsx (Vite/bundler). For Next.js app router, import it from the root layout instead.

slots.name: brand
slots.purpose: Product name + optional tagline in the header.

slots.name: nav-links
slots.purpose: Primary navigation links inside the NavBar. Plain NavBar.Link anchors by default; router apps use the asChild recipe in the slot comment.

slots.name: header-actions
slots.purpose: Right-side header controls. Defaults to the exported ThemeToggle.

slots.name: main-content
slots.purpose: The page body. Defaults to rendering the children prop; router apps put their outlet here.

slots.name: theme-storage-key
slots.purpose: The localStorage key persisting the theme choice.

slots.name: app-custom-styles
slots.purpose: App-added chrome rules appended after the template's.

setup: import '@trembus/ui/styles.css' once at the app entry (before app css)
render <AppShell> at the app root; it supplies the .tcl-root wrapper itself
theme is data-theme on <html> — set an initial value in index.html to avoid a flash if desired

changes: Initial template: SkipLink + sticky header (brand/nav/actions) + main + footer, ThemeToggle + SSR-safe useTheme, tokens-only chrome css.
